qa_pairs.question: What are the group name conventions for different Resource Allocation Projects (RAPs)?
qa_pairs.answer: For Default RAPs, the convention is `def-[profname][-xx]`. For RRG/HPC resource RAPs, it's `rrg-[profname][-xx]`. For RPP/HPC resource RAPs, it's `rpp-[profname][-xx]`. For RRG/Cloud resource RAPs, it's `crg-[profname][-xx]`. For RPP/Cloud resource RAPs, it's `cpp-[profname][-xx]`.

qa_pairs.question: How is the group name used in the system?
qa_pairs.answer: The group name is used as a POSIX group name with an associated POSIX group ID and is propagated through LDAP in the `dn` attribute, for example: `dn: cn=rpp-profname,ou=Group,dc=computecanada,dc=ca`.

qa_pairs.question: Who has access to a PI's Default RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: All of a PI's activated sponsored user roles are always members of the PI's default RAP. Confirming sponsorship of a user confers on them membership in a PI's default RAP, and this cannot be modified directly.

qa_pairs.question: Can a PI modify membership in their Default RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: Membership in a PI's default RAP cannot be modified directly. However, a PI can deactivate any role they sponsor at any time.

qa_pairs.question: How does membership work for a RAC RAP with HPC resources?
qa_pairs.answer: When a new RAC RAP is created with HPC resources, CCDB automatically adds all of the PI's sponsored user roles, all associated Co-PI roles, and all sponsored user roles of all associated Co-PIs as members. Any new role sponsored by the PI after the RAP's creation will also be automatically added.

qa_pairs.question: Who is initially a member of a RAC RAP with Cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: When a new RAC RAP is created with Cloud resources allocated, only the PI is initially added as a member.

qa_pairs.question: Who can manage membership for a RAC RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI can modify the membership of any of their RAC RAPs at any time.

qa_pairs.question: Who can be added as a member to a RAC RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: Any user with an active Alliance account can be added as a member of a RAC RAP, including user roles the PI is not sponsoring (e.g., a co-PI).

qa_pairs.question: What are the three RAP membership permission levels?
qa_pairs.answer: There are three RAP membership permission levels: Owner, Manager, and Member.

qa_pairs.question: What is the role of an 'Owner' in RAP membership?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI is the sole Owner of the RAP and all associated allocations, and this cannot be changed. The Owner can add or remove RAP managers and members.

qa_pairs.question: What are the permissions of a 'Manager' for a RAC RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: A Manager has elevated permission (on CCDB only, not on the clusters) delegated by the owner or another manager that allows making membership changes. Managers can also use the PI's allocation in the corresponding cluster(s).

qa_pairs.question: What are the limitations of a 'Manager' in a RAC RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: Members promoted to Manager on a RAC RAP cannot make changes on the project/nearline file systems on behalf of the PIs, such as changing file ownership, applying ACLs, or managing permissions on Globus.

qa_pairs.question: What can a 'Member' do in a RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: Members can use the PI’s allocation in the corresponding cluster(s) but cannot make any modifications to the RAP membership.

qa_pairs.question: How is RAP membership represented in LDAP?
qa_pairs.answer: RAP membership is represented as a group in LDAP. It defines a group of users that are authorized to submit jobs against the RAPI (which is the ID of the RAP) and share files within the same Unix group.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find detailed instructions on adding members to a RAC RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: For detailed instructions on how to add members to a RAC RAP, please visit the 'Using a resource allocation' page under the 'Information on each resource' section.

qa_pairs.question: Can I sign up for a new Alliance account if I already have one but my position or sponsor has changed?
qa_pairs.answer: No, each person can only have one Alliance account (one CCI), and requests for duplicate CCIs are refused. If your position or sponsor has changed, you should apply for a new role instead, which will have a new CCRI.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I have an existing Alliance account and my position or sponsor has changed?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have an existing CCI and have changed position or sponsor, you should apply for a new role (which will have a new CCRI). To do so, log in with your existing account and visit the 'apply for a new role' form on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I've forgotten my Alliance account password?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have forgotten your password, you can reset it by visiting the password reset link on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: What if I can no longer access the email address associated with my Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: If you can no longer access the email address you have on file, you should email `accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca` and they can update it for you.
